data.body:
Root-caused and fixed — but the fix INVERTS the ticket's proposed direction, per an explicit design ruling that a dependency change must invalidate a review.

Investigation (reproduced live on origin/main, post-d70a):
- d70a (7c3c08df8) already scoped store-WIDE/UNRELATED writes out of PlanReviewGeneration identity, so truly-unrelated concurrent writes no longer abort signing.
- The observed epic+children parallel-replan failures were NOT unrelated writes — they were changes to the epic's DIRECT related material (child pins), which sign_manifest deliberately (and correctly) invalidates.

Reframe (design ruling): a dependency (child/prerequisite) changing after a review INVALIDATES it — a plan review binds its dependencies' material. So the epic-review invalidations were CORRECT behavior, not the bug. The real defect was the OPPOSITE of the ticket's premise: `rebar sign-review` (resign_plan_review) re-certified across a dependency change because its dependency-pin staleness check was gated behind verify.enforce_plan_material_pins (default False). sign_manifest (review-time) always enforces it; sign-review (recovery) did not — so sign-review could BYPASS the invalidation. That inconsistency is the bug.

Fix:
1. resign_plan_review now invalidates on a positively-detected dependency drift/loss (stale-pin-drift/stale-pin-missing) UNCONDITIONALLY, regardless of enforce_plan_material_pins — aligning sign-review with sign_manifest.
2. sign_manifest's abort message now names WHICH material changed (own vs a dependency) — satisfies AC "message naming the ticket-material change".
3. The residual CLI message differentiates: a genuine material change → "run `rebar review-plan`" (re-review IS required; the recorded review is stale); a TRANSIENT nothing-changed failure → "run `rebar sign-review <id>`" (cheap, no LLM re-run).
4. Docs/guides/help updated to prescribe dependency-graph-order review (docs/plan-review-gate.md §"Review dependencies FIRST", the packaged `rebar explain plan` guide, and AGENTS.md) — reviewing a ticket and its dependencies in parallel triggers this invalidation and must be avoided.

AC note: original criterion 3 ("message points to sign-review, not re-review") was based on the misdiagnosis that sign-review legitimately certifies these. Corrected: for a genuine dependency/own-material change, re-review IS required and sign-review correctly refuses; sign-review is pointed to only for transient (nothing-changed) failures. Criteria 1 (unrelated→certified) and 2 (own-material change→fail closed, named) hold.

Tests: tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_plan_review_dependency_invalidation_790c.py (held-out, RED→GREEN, mutation-checked). make lint + typecheck green; 880 passed in the plan-review/resign/attest sweep.
